title: Electricity
summary: Chapter 11 covers electric charge, current, potential difference, resistance, and electric circuits. Ohm's Law relates voltage, current, and resistance. Resistances in series and parallel are calculated. Heating effect of electric current (Joule's Law) explains how electrical appliances work. Electric power and energy are calculated. The domestic electric circuit including earthing, fuse, and safety measures are explained.
learningPoints: Define electric charge, current (I = Q/t), and potential difference
State Ohm's Law: V = IR at constant temperature
Define resistance and state factors affecting it: length, area, temperature, material
Calculate resistance in series: R = R₁ + R₂ + R₃
State Joule's Law of heating: H = I²Rt
Calculate electric power: P = VI = I²R = V²/R
Calculate electric energy: E = Pt (kWh for billing)
Draw and interpret circuit diagrams with symbols
Explain the domestic electric circuit: live, neutral, earth wires
Explain the need for fuse and MCB in circuits
List precautions to prevent overloading in domestic circuits

laws.name: Ohm's Law
laws.statement: The potential difference across the ends of a metallic conductor is directly proportional to the current flowing through it at constant temperature. V ∝ I, or V = IR.

laws.name: Joule's Law of Heating
laws.statement: The heat produced in a resistor is directly proportional to: (i) square of current (H ∝ I²) (ii) resistance (H ∝ R) (iii) time (H ∝ t). So H = I²Rt.

formulas.label: Ohm's Law
formulas.description: V = potential difference (volts), I = current (amperes), R = resistance (ohms).

formulas.label: Electric Current
formulas.description: Current = charge per unit time. SI unit: Ampere (A). 1A = 1 Coulomb/second.

formulas.label: Resistance in Series
formulas.description: Total resistance in series = sum of individual resistances. Current same through all.

formulas.label: Resistance in Parallel
formulas.description: Total resistance in parallel less than smallest individual resistance. Voltage same across all.

formulas.label: Joule's Law
formulas.description: Heat produced in a resistor in time t.

formulas.label: Electric Power
formulas.description: Power = rate of energy consumption. Unit: Watt (W).

formulas.label: Electric Energy (Commercial)
formulas.description: 1 unit of electricity = 1 kWh. Used for billing.

formulas.label: Resistivity
formulas.description: R depends on length (L), cross-sectional area (A) and material (ρ = resistivity).

diagrams.title: Simple Electric Circuit
diagrams.description: Battery (cell), switch (key), ammeter (in series, measures current), voltmeter (in parallel, measures PD), resistor, connecting wires. Direction of current: + terminal to - terminal (conventional).

diagrams.title: Series vs Parallel Circuits
diagrams.description: Series: components end-to-end, same current through all, voltages add. Parallel: components side-by-side, same voltage across all, currents add.

diagrams.title: Domestic Electric Circuit
diagrams.description: Live wire (red/brown, 220V), Neutral wire (black/blue, 0V), Earth wire (green, safety). Main switch, fuse/MCB, individual switches for each appliance.

resources.label: Karnataka SSLC Science Textbook
resources.description: Official Karnataka SSLC Science textbook Chapter 11 Electricity.

resources.label: Electricity — Video Lessons
resources.description: Khan Academy videos on Ohm's law, circuits, power and heating effect.

pyqs.question: SI unit of potential difference is: (A) volt (B) ampere (C) coulomb (D) ohm meter
pyqs.answer: (A) Volt (V). Potential difference is measured in volts. 1V = 1 J/C.

pyqs.question: Mention factors on which resistance of a conductor depend.
pyqs.answer: (i) Length of conductor — longer = more resistance (ii) Area of cross-section — wider = less resistance (iii) Nature/material of conductor (resistivity ρ) (iv) Temperature — higher temp = higher resistance (for metals).

pyqs.question: TV (250W, 4hrs) vs Toaster (1200W, 1hr). Which consumes more energy? Also: 2Ω and 4Ω in parallel. If 6Ω replaces 4Ω, find difference in total resistance.
pyqs.answer: TV: E = 250×4 = 1000Wh = 1kWh. Toaster: E = 1200×1 = 1200Wh = 1.2kWh. Toaster consumes more. Parallel 2Ω+4Ω: 1/R = 1/2+1/4 = 3/4; R₁ = 4/3 = 1.33Ω. Parallel 2Ω+6Ω: 1/R = 1/2+1/6 = 4/6; R₂ = 6/4 = 1.5Ω. Difference = 1.5-1.33 = 0.17Ω.

pyqs.question: SI unit of electric current is: (A) ampere (B) ohm (C) volt (D) watt
pyqs.answer: (A) Ampere (A). Current I = Q/t. 1 Ampere = 1 Coulomb per second.

pyqs.question: Draw a simple electric circuit. Label ammeter and voltmeter.
pyqs.answer: Circuit: Battery → Key → Ammeter (in series) → Resistor → back to battery. Voltmeter connected in parallel across resistor. Ammeter symbol: (A), Voltmeter symbol: (V).

pyqs.question: Electric motor: 10A current from 220V source. Find power.
State Joule's law. Name four appliances working on heating effect. Why is tungsten used in bulbs? Why copper/aluminium for transmission?
pyqs.answer: Joule's law: H = I²Rt. Appliances: iron box, geyser, oven, toaster. Tungsten: high resistivity and very high melting point (3380°C). Copper/Al: very low resistivity (good conductors) for efficient transmission.

mcqs.question: SI unit of potential difference is:
mcqs.explanation: Potential difference is measured in Volts (V). 1V = 1 J/C. It drives current through a circuit.

mcqs.question: Ohm's Law states:
options: A. V ∝ R at constant I
B. V ∝ I at constant T and R
C. I ∝ R at constant V
mcqs.explanation: Ohm's Law: V ∝ I at constant temperature. The constant of proportionality is resistance R. V = IR.

mcqs.question: Resistance in series of R₁=2Ω and R₂=4Ω is:
mcqs.explanation: Series: R = R₁ + R₂ = 2 + 4 = 6Ω. In series, total resistance is always greater than largest individual resistance.

mcqs.question: Resistance in parallel of 2Ω and 4Ω is:
mcqs.explanation: 1/R = 1/2 + 1/4 = 2/4 + 1/4 = 3/4. R = 4/3 = 1.33Ω. Parallel resistance is less than smallest.

mcqs.question: Fan with power 880W connected to 220V. Current is:
mcqs.explanation: P = VI. I = P/V = 880/220 = 4A. Resistance = V/I = 220/4 = 55Ω.

mcqs.question: Joule's law states heat produced is proportional to:
options: B. Square of current × resistance × time
mcqs.explanation: H = I²Rt. Joule's law: heat ∝ I², heat ∝ R, heat ∝ t.

mcqs.question: Tungsten is used in electric bulb filaments because of:
options: B. High resistivity and very high melting point
mcqs.explanation: Tungsten: high resistivity (produces heat and light) and very high melting point (3380°C — doesn't melt even at white hot temperature).

mcqs.question: 1 kWh equals:
mcqs.explanation: 1 kWh = 1000W × 3600s = 3,600,000 J = 3.6 × 10⁶ J. This is the commercial unit of electrical energy.

mcqs.question: Toaster (1200W, 1hr) vs TV (250W, 4hrs). Which consumes more energy?
mcqs.explanation: Toaster: 1200×1 = 1200 Wh = 1.2 kWh. TV: 250×4 = 1000 Wh = 1 kWh. Toaster consumes more.

mcqs.question: The potential difference between live and neutral wire in domestic circuit is:
mcqs.explanation: In India, domestic supply: Live wire at 220V, Neutral wire at 0V. Potential difference = 220V, frequency = 50Hz.

mcqs.question: Factors affecting resistance include all EXCEPT:
mcqs.explanation: Resistance depends on length (L), area (A), material (ρ), temperature. Colour has no effect on electrical resistance.

mcqs.question: Precautions to avoid overload in domestic circuit:
options: A. Use appliances of high wattage
B. Connect many appliances to one socket
C. Avoid direct contact of live and neutral wires
D. Remove fuse for better conduction
mcqs.explanation: Overload prevention: live and neutral wires should not contact each other, use appropriate fuse rating, don't overload sockets, maintain insulation.

mcqs.question: Ammeter is connected in circuit:
mcqs.explanation: Ammeter (very low resistance) is connected in series to measure current without reducing it. Voltmeter (very high resistance) is connected in parallel.

mcqs.question: Electric power formula is:
mcqs.explanation: P = VI = I²R = V²/R. Power in watts = current in amps × voltage in volts.

mcqs.question: The statement 'potential difference between two points is 1V' means:
options: A. 1 joule of work done in moving 1 coulomb of charge
mcqs.explanation: 1 Volt = 1 Joule per Coulomb. Moving 1C of charge between two points requires 1J of work if PD is 1V.

exercises.question: A 350W toaster (6hrs/day) and 250W iron (4hrs/day) are used for 30 days. Find total energy cost at ₹4/kWh.
State Ohm's Law. Draw a circuit to verify it.
exercises.answer: Ohm's Law: V/I = constant = R (at constant temperature). Circuit: Battery + rheostat (to vary current) + ammeter (in series) + resistor under test + voltmeter (in parallel across resistor). Record V and I for different rheostat settings. V/I = constant confirms Ohm's Law.

exercises.question: 2Ω and 4Ω are in parallel. If 4Ω is replaced by 6Ω, find difference in total resistance.
State Joule's Law. Why is tungsten used in bulbs and copper in wires?
exercises.answer: Joule's Law: H = I²Rt. Tungsten: high resistivity (glows hot, produces light) + very high melting point (3380°C, doesn't melt). Copper: very low resistivity → low resistance → conducts electricity efficiently with minimum heat loss.

exercises.question: What precautions prevent overload in domestic circuits?
exercises.answer: (i) Live and neutral wires must not touch directly (ii) Insulation on wires must be intact (iii) No fault in electrical appliances (iv) Do not connect many appliances to one socket (v) Use correct fuse rating for each circuit.

exercises.question: Three resistors 5Ω, 10Ω, 15Ω connected in series to 6V battery. Find (i) total resistance (ii) current through circuit (iii) voltage across each resistor.
Explain the domestic electric circuit with the role of earthing and fuse.
exercises.answer: Live wire (220V), Neutral (0V), Earth wire (safety). Fuse: thin wire that melts if current exceeds rated value — breaks circuit preventing damage. MCB: modern switch that trips automatically. Earthing: connects metal bodies of appliances to earth — if fault occurs, current flows to earth (not through person).
